# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as parent image 
FROM node:14 AS builder   # Step 2 & 3 - Building the React app and creating a Docker Image for it (Step A)
WORKDIR /app    # Set working directory to '/app' in step B, C or E below this line. If not specified then current folder is used as workdir by default which can lead into errors if any command tries to write files outside of the container’s filesystem – Step D and F above
# Copy package*.json file from /src directory (Step A)   # Copies all packages listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
COPY package*.json ./    # Copying all packages listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
# Install any needed dependencies (Step A)   # Copies all packages listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
RUN npm install    # Runs 'npm' command and downloads all dependencies listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
# Copy source code from /src directory (Step A)   # Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
COPY . ./    # Copying source code from current directory into Docker container (Step A)   - Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
# Build React App (Step A)   # Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
RUN npm run build    # Runs 'npm' command and downloads all dependencies listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
# Serve React App (Step A)   # Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
FROM nginx:1.20    # Use NGINX version (Step A)   - Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
COPY --from=builder /app/build/. .    # Copying all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
EXPOSE 80    # Expose port (Step A)   - Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
ENTRYPOINT ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]    # Entry point (Step A)   - Copies all files listed in "package.json" inside your docker image, so that they are included when you build an application using this nodejs app as a base for another project! This is step B – Step C below if we want to use the same package list
